text: A magnitude 6.2 earthquake struck central Italy on Wednesday, causing widespread damage to buildings in Amatrice. Rescue teams recovered 12 survivors from the rubble. A 4.5 magnitude aftershock hit the same region the following day.
Police arrested three suspects in connection with the robbery of a downtown jewelry store. The heist, which occurred last Tuesday, resulted in the theft of over $500,000 in merchandise.

Two separate car accidents on Highway 101 caused major traffic delays during the morning commute. The first collision involved three vehicles near the Elm Street exit, while a second accident occurred two miles north involving a delivery truck and a sedan.

The tech company announced layoffs affecting 2,000 employees across three offices. The restructuring follows last quarter's disappointing earnings report and a failed merger attempt with a rival firm.
Heavy flooding in the river valley displaced over 5,000 residents. Emergency shelters were set up in four local schools. The governor declared a state of emergency and requested federal assistance.
A shooting at the shopping mall left two people injured. Police responded within minutes and apprehended the suspect after a brief chase through the parking lot.

The factory fire broke out at approximately 3 AM and burned for six hours before firefighters brought it under control. No injuries were reported, but the facility sustained millions in damage. An investigation into the cause has been opened.

Protesters gathered outside city hall to demand action on climate change. Later that evening, a separate group marched to the state capitol building. Police reported two arrests at the second demonstration.
The hurricane made landfall near Galveston with winds of 130 mph, causing extensive flooding and power outages affecting 200,000 homes.

Three bombs exploded in different parts of the city within a 30-minute window. The first targeted a government building, the second hit a market, and the third struck near a bus station. Authorities believe the attacks were coordinated.
